title: Armor, +3
description: You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.

title: Adamantine Armor
description: This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.

title: Mithral Armor
description: Mithral is a light, flexible metal. A mithral chain shirt or breastplate can be worn under normal clothes. If the armor normally imposes disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks or has a Strength requirement, the mithral version of the armor doesn't.

title: Armor of Resistance
description: You have resistance to one type of damage while you wear this armor. The DM chooses the type or determines it randomly from: acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, necrotic, poison, psychic, radiant, or thunder.

title: Mariner's Armor
description: While wearing this armor, you have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed. In addition, whenever you start your turn underwater with 0 hit points, the armor causes you to rise 60 feet toward the surface.

title: Cast-Off Armor
description: You can doff this armor as an action.

title: Smoldering Armor
description: Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.

title: Padded
description: AC 11 + Dex modifier. Cost: 5 gp. Weight: 8 lb. Disadvantage on Stealth checks.

title: Leather
description: AC 11 + Dex modifier. Cost: 10 gp. Weight: 10 lb.

title: Studded Leather
description: AC 12 + Dex modifier. Cost: 45 gp. Weight: 13 lb.

title: Glamoured Studded Leather
description: While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC. You can also use a bonus action to speak the armor's command word and cause the armor to assume the appearance of a normal set of clothing or some other kind of armor. The illusory appearance lasts until you use this property again or remove the armor.

title: Hide
description: AC 12 + Dex modifier (max 2). Cost: 10 gp. Weight: 12 lb.

title: Chain Shirt
description: AC 13 + Dex modifier (max 2). Cost: 50 gp. Weight: 20 lb.

title: Elven Chain
description: You gain a +1 bonus to AC while you wear this armor. You are considered proficient with this armor even if you lack proficiency with medium armor.

title: Scale Mail
description: AC 14 + Dex modifier (max 2). Cost: 50 gp. Weight: 45 lb. Disadvantage on Stealth checks.

title: Dragon Scale Mail
description: Dragon scale mail is made of the scales of one kind of dragon. You gain a +1 bonus to AC, advantage on saving throws against the Frightful Presence and breath weapons of dragons, and resistance to one damage type determined by the kind of dragon that provided the scales. Additionally, you can use an action to magically discern the distance and direction to the closest dragon of that kind within 30 miles of you.

title: Breastplate
description: AC 14 + Dex modifier (max 2). Cost: 400 gp. Weight: 20 lb.

title: Half Plate
description: AC 15 + Dex modifier (max 2). Cost: 750 gp. Weight: 40 lb. Disadvantage on Stealth checks.

title: Ring Mail
description: AC 14. Cost: 30 gp. Weight: 40 lb. Disadvantage on Stealth checks.

title: Chain Mail
description: AC 16. Cost: 75 gp. Weight: 55 lb. Requires Strength 13. Disadvantage on Stealth checks.

title: Efreeti Chain
description: While wearing this armor, you gain a +3 bonus to AC, you are immune to fire damage, and you can understand and speak Ignan. In addition, you can stand on and walk across molten rock as if it were solid ground.

title: Splint
description: AC 17. Cost: 200 gp. Weight: 60 lb. Requires Strength 15. Disadvantage on Stealth checks.

title: Plate
description: AC 18. Cost: 1,500 gp. Weight: 65 lb. Requires Strength 15. Disadvantage on Stealth checks.

title: Dwarven Plate
description: While wearing this armor, you gain a +2 bonus to AC. In addition, if an effect moves you against your will along the ground, you can use your reaction to reduce the distance you are moved by up to 10 feet.

title: Plate Armor of Etherealness
description: While you're wearing this armor, you can speak its command word as an action to gain the effect of the etherealness spell, which lasts for 10 minutes or until you remove the armor or use an action to speak the command word again. This property of the armor can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Demon Armor
description: While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC, and you can understand and speak Abyssal. The armor's clawed gauntlets turn unarmed strikes into magic weapons that deal 1d8 slashing damage. The armor has a curse: once you don it, you can't doff it unless you are targeted by a remove curse spell or similar magic, and you have disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws against demons.

title: Armor of Invulnerability
description: You have resistance to nonmagical damage while you wear this armor. Additionally, you can use an action to make yourself immune to nonmagical damage for 10 minutes or until you are no longer wearing the armor. Once this special action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Armor of Vulnerability
description: While wearing this armor, you have resistance to one of the following damage types: bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing. The DM chooses the type or determines it randomly. The armor has a curse: wearing it gives you vulnerability to two of the three damage types (the ones not chosen for resistance), which is hidden from you.

title: Shield
description: A shield is made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your Armor Class by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time. Cost: 10 gp. Weight: 6 lb.

title: Shield, +1
description: While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.

title: Shield, +2
description: While holding this shield, you have a +2 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.

title: Shield, +3
description: While holding this shield, you have a +3 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.

title: Animated Shield
description: While holding this shield, you can speak its command word as a bonus action to cause it to animate. The shield leaps into the air and hovers in your space to protect you as if you were wielding it, leaving your hands free. The shield remains animated for 1 minute, until you use a bonus action to end this effect, or until you are incapacitated or die, at which point the shield falls to the ground or into your hand if you have one free.

title: Arrow-Catching Shield
description: You gain a +2 bonus to AC against ranged attacks while you wield this shield. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC. In addition, whenever an attacker makes a ranged attack against a target within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to become the target of the attack instead.

title: Sentinel Shield
description: While holding this shield, you have advantage on initiative rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks. The shield is emblazoned with a symbol of an eye.

title: Shield of Missile Attraction
description: While holding this shield, you have resistance to damage from ranged weapon attacks. The shield is cursed: attuning to it curses you until you are targeted by a remove curse spell or similar magic. Whenever a ranged weapon attack is made against a target within 10 feet of you, the curse causes you to become the target instead.

title: Spellguard Shield
description: While holding this shield, you have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects, and spell attacks have disadvantage against you.

title: Shield of Expression
description: The front of this shield is shaped in the likeness of a face. While bearing the shield, you can use a bonus action to alter the face's expression.

title: Circlet of Blasting
description: While wearing this circlet, you can use an action to cast the scorching ray spell with it. When you make the spell's attacks, you do so with an attack bonus of +5. The circlet can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

title: Eyes of Charming
description: These crystal lenses fit over the eyes. They have 3 charges. While wearing them, you can expend 1 charge as an action to cast the charm person spell (save DC 13) on a humanoid within 30 feet of you. The lenses regain all expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Eyes of Minute Seeing
description: These crystal lenses fit over the eyes. While wearing them, you can see much better than normal out to a range of 1 foot. You have advantage on Intelligence (Investigation) checks that rely on sight while searching an area or studying an object within that range.

title: Eyes of the Eagle
description: These crystal lenses fit over the eyes. While wearing them, you have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight. In conditions of clear visibility, you can make out details of even extremely distant creatures and objects as small as 2 feet across.

title: Hat of Disguise
description: While wearing this hat, you can use an action to cast the disguise self spell from it at will. The spell ends if the hat is removed.

title: Hat of Vermin
description: This hat has 3 charges. While holding the hat, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges and speak a command word that summons your choice of a bat, a frog, or a rat. The summoned creature magically appears in the hat and tries to get away from you as quickly as possible. It is an ordinary animal, not combative, and it disappears after 1 hour or when reduced to 0 hit points. The hat regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Hat of Wizardry
description: This antiquated, cone-shaped hat is adorned with gold crescent moons and stars. While you are wearing it, you gain the following benefits: you can use the hat as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells, and you can try to cast a cantrip that you don't know by succeeding on a DC 10 Intelligence (Arcana) check; on a failed check the spell fails with no effect. The hat has 3 charges, and you expend 1 charge each time you attempt to cast a cantrip this way. The hat regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Headband of Intellect
description: Your Intelligence score is 19 while you wear this headband. It has no effect on you if your Intelligence is already 19 or higher.

title: Helm of Brilliance
description: This dazzling helm is set with 1d10 diamonds, 2d10 rubies, 3d10 fire opals, and 5d10 opals. Gems can be used to cast various spells: daylight (opal), fireball (fire opal), prismatic spray (diamond), or wall of fire (ruby). You gain resistance to fire damage, and you can use an action to cast the light cantrip from a remaining diamond. When all gems are used or destroyed, the helm loses its magic.

title: Helm of Comprehending Languages
description: While wearing this helm, you can use an action to cast the comprehend languages spell from it at will.

title: Helm of Telepathy
description: While wearing this helm, you can use an action to cast the detect thoughts spell (save DC 13) from it. As long as you maintain concentration on the spell, you can use a bonus action to send a telepathic message to a creature you are focused on, and it can reply telepathically. While focusing on a creature with detect thoughts, you can use an action to cast the suggestion spell (save DC 13) on that creature, after which the detect thoughts spell ends.

title: Helm of Teleportation
description: This helm has 3 charges. While wearing it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to cast the teleport spell from it. The helm regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Dread Helm
description: This fearsome steel helm makes your eyes glow red while you wear it.

title: Amulet of Proof against Detection and Location
filename: Amulet of Proof against Detection and Location
description: While wearing this amulet, you are hidden from divination magic. You can't be targeted by such magic or perceived through magical scrying sensors.

title: Amulet of the Planes
description: While wearing this amulet, you can use an action to name a location that you are familiar with on another plane of existence, then make a DC 15 Intelligence check. On a success, you cast the plane shift spell. On a failure, you and each creature and object within 15 feet of you travel to a random destination.

title: Clockwork Amulet
description: This copper amulet contains tiny interlocking gears and is powered by magic from Mechanus. When you make an attack roll while wearing the amulet, you can forgo rolling the d20 to get a 10 on the die. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Dark Shard Amulet
description: This amulet is fashioned from a single shard of resilient extraplanar material originating from the realm of your warlock patron. While you are wearing it, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells, and you can try to cast a cantrip that you don't know by succeeding on a DC 10 Intelligence (Arcana) check.

title: Amulet of the Devout, +1
description: This amulet bears the symbol of a deity inlaid with precious stones or metals. While you wear this holy symbol, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells. While holding the amulet, you can use it as a spellcasting focus and you can use your Channel Divinity feature one additional time between rests.

title: Amulet of the Devout, +2
description: This amulet bears the symbol of a deity inlaid with precious stones or metals. While you wear this holy symbol, you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells. While holding the amulet, you can use it as a spellcasting focus and you can use your Channel Divinity feature one additional time between rests.

title: Amulet of the Devout, +3
description: This amulet bears the symbol of a deity inlaid with precious stones or metals. While you wear this holy symbol, you gain a +3 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells. While holding the amulet, you can use it as a spellcasting focus and you can use your Channel Divinity feature one additional time between rests.

title: Talisman of Pure Good
attunement: by a creature of good alignment
description: This talisman is a mighty symbol of goodness. A creature that is neither good nor evil in alignment takes 6d6 radiant damage upon touching the talisman. The talisman has 7 charges. As an action, you can expend 1 charge and target one creature on the ground that you can see within 120 feet of you, opening a flaming fissure beneath it. The target must succeed on a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw or fall into the fissure and be destroyed, leaving no remains.

title: Talisman of the Sphere
description: When you make an Intelligence (Arcana) check to control a sphere of annihilation, you do so with double your proficiency bonus on the check. In addition, when you start your turn with control over a sphere of annihilation, you can use an action to levitate it 10 feet plus a number of additional feet equal to 10 times your Intelligence modifier.

title: Talisman of Ultimate Evil
attunement: by a creature of evil alignment
description: This item symbolizes unrepentant evil. A creature that is neither good nor evil in alignment takes 6d6 necrotic damage upon touching the talisman. The talisman has 6 charges. As an action, you can expend 1 charge and target one creature on the ground that you can see within 120 feet of you, opening a flaming fissure beneath it. The target must succeed on a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw or fall into the fissure and be destroyed, leaving no remains.

title: Brooch of Shielding
description: While wearing this brooch, you have resistance to force damage, and you have immunity to damage from the magic missile spell.

title: Scarab of Protection
description: This scarab-shaped brooch has 12 charges and gives you advantage on saving throws against spells. If you succeed on a saving throw against a necromancy spell or a harmful effect originating from an undead creature, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge to turn the effect back on the caster, who must make its own saving throw against the spell or effect.

title: Medallion of Thoughts
description: This medallion has 3 charges. While wearing it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to cast the detect thoughts spell (save DC 13) from it. The medallion regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Necklace of Adaptation
description: While wearing this necklace, you can breathe normally in any environment, and you have advantage on saving throws made against harmful gases and vapors such as cloudkill and stinking cloud effects, inhaled poisons, and the breath weapons of some dragons.

title: Necklace of Fireballs
description: This necklace has 1d6 + 3 beads hanging from it. You can use an action to detach a bead and throw it up to 60 feet away, where it detonates as a 3rd-level fireball spell (save DC 15). You can hurl multiple beads as part of the same action, increasing the level of the fireball by 1 for each bead beyond the first.

title: Necklace of Prayer Beads
attunement: by a Cleric, Druid, or Paladin
description: This necklace has 1d4 + 2 magic beads made from aquamarine, black pearl, or topaz. Each bead has a specific magical property that can be activated as a bonus action, allowing you to cast spells such as bless, cure wounds, lesser restoration, greater restoration, branding smite, planar ally, or wind walk depending on the bead type.

title: Periapt of Health
description: You are immune to contracting any disease while you wear this pendant. If you are already infected with a disease, the effects of the disease are suppressed while you wear the pendant.

title: Periapt of Proof against Poison
description: This delicate silver chain has a brilliant-cut black gem pendant. While you wear it, poisons have no effect on you. You are immune to the poisoned condition and have immunity to poison damage.

title: Periapt of Wound Closure
description: While you wear this pendant, you stabilize whenever you are dying at the start of your turn. In addition, whenever you roll a Hit Die to regain hit points, double the number of hit points it restores.

title: Ring of Animal Influence
description: This ring has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the ring, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to cast animal friendship (save DC 13), fear (save DC 13) targeting only beasts, or speak with animals from the ring.

title: Ring of Djinni Summoning
description: While wearing this ring, you can speak its command word as an action to summon a particular djinni from the Elemental Plane of Air. The djinni appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 120 feet of you and remains as long as you concentrate, up to 1 hour. After the djinni departs, it can't be summoned again for 24 hours, and the ring becomes nonmagical if the djinni dies.

title: Ring of Elemental Command (Air)
description: While wearing this ring, you have advantage on attack rolls against elementals from the Elemental Plane of Air, and they have disadvantage on attack rolls against you. You can also speak and understand Auran. You have access to properties that include casting wind wall, dominate monster on air elementals, chain lightning, and gust of wind at will, along with resistance to lightning damage and the ability to fly.

title: Ring of Elemental Command (Earth)
description: While wearing this ring, you have advantage on attack rolls against elementals from the Elemental Plane of Earth, and they have disadvantage on attack rolls against you. You can also speak and understand Terran. You have access to properties that include casting stone shape, stoneskin, wall of stone, dominate monster on earth elementals, and move earth, along with resistance to acid damage.

title: Ring of Elemental Command (Fire)
description: While wearing this ring, you have advantage on attack rolls against elementals from the Elemental Plane of Fire, and they have disadvantage on attack rolls against you. You can also speak and understand Ignan. You have access to properties that include casting burning hands, fireball, wall of fire, dominate monster on fire elementals, and fire shield, along with immunity to fire damage.

title: Ring of Elemental Command (Water)
description: While wearing this ring, you have advantage on attack rolls against elementals from the Elemental Plane of Water, and they have disadvantage on attack rolls against you. You can also speak and understand Aquan. You have access to properties that include casting create or destroy water, control water, ice storm, wall of ice, and dominate monster on water elementals, along with the ability to breathe underwater and swim.

title: Ring of Evasion
description: This ring has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. When you fail a Dexterity saving throw while wearing it, you can use your reaction to expend 1 of its charges to succeed on that saving throw instead.

title: Ring of Feather Falling
description: When you fall while wearing this ring, you descend 60 feet per round and take no damage from falling.

title: Ring of Free Action
description: While you wear this ring, difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement. In addition, magic can neither reduce your speed nor cause you to be paralyzed or restrained.

title: Ring of Invisibility
description: While wearing this ring, you can turn invisible as an action. Anything you are wearing or carrying is invisible with you. You remain invisible until the ring is removed, until you attack or cast a spell, or until you use a bonus action to become visible again.

title: Ring of Jumping
description: While wearing this ring, you can cast the jump spell from it as a bonus action at will, but can target only yourself when you do so.

title: Ring of Mind Shielding
description: While wearing this ring, you are immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, know your alignment, or know your creature type. Creatures can telepathically communicate with you only if you allow it. If you die while wearing the ring, your soul enters it unless it already houses a soul.

title: Ring of Regeneration
description: While wearing this ring, you regain 1d6 hit points every 10 minutes, provided that you have at least 1 hit point. If you lose a body part, the ring causes the missing part to regrow and return to full functionality after 1d6 + 1 days if you have at least 1 hit point the whole time.

title: Ring of Resistance
description: You have resistance to one damage type while wearing this ring. The gem in the ring indicates the type, which the DM chooses or determines randomly. The damage types and their associated gemstones include pearl (acid), tourmaline (cold), garnet (fire), sapphire (force), topaz (lightning), jet (necrotic), amethyst (poison), jade (psychic), citrine (radiant), jacinth (thunder).

title: Ring of Shooting Stars
description: While wearing this ring in dim light or darkness, you can cast dancing lights and light from the ring at will. The ring has 6 charges and regains 1d6 charges daily at dawn. You can expend charges to use the Faerie Fire, Ball Lightning, or Shooting Stars properties.

title: Ring of Spell Turning
description: While wearing this ring, you have advantage on saving throws against any spell that targets only you (not in an area of effect). In addition, if you roll a 20 for the save and the spell is 7th level or lower, the spell has no effect on you and instead targets the caster, using the slot level, spell save DC, attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the caster.

title: Ring of Swimming
description: You have a swimming speed of 40 feet while wearing this ring.

title: Ring of Telekinesis
description: While wearing this ring, you can cast the telekinesis spell at will, but you can target only objects that aren't being worn or carried.

title: Ring of the Ram
description: This ring has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the ring, you can use an action to expend 1 to 3 of its charges to make a ranged spell attack against one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The ring produces a spectral ram's head that deals 2d10 force damage per charge spent and can push the target 5 feet away per charge.

title: Ring of Three Wishes
description: While wearing this ring, you can use an action to expend 1 of its 3 charges to cast the wish spell from it. The ring becomes nonmagical when you use the last charge.

title: Ring of Warmth
description: While wearing this ring, you have resistance to cold damage. In addition, you and everything you wear and carry are unharmed by temperatures as low as -50 degrees Fahrenheit.

title: Ring of Water Walking
description: While wearing this ring, you can stand on and move across any liquid surface as if it were solid ground.

title: Ring of X-ray Vision
description: While wearing this ring, you can use an action to speak its command word and gain the ability to see into and through solid matter for 1 minute. This vision has a range of 30 feet. To you, solid objects within that range appear transparent. The vision can penetrate 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, or up to 3 feet of wood or dirt. Thicker substances or a thin sheet of lead block the vision.

title: Belt of Dwarvenkind
description: While wearing this belt, you gain a +2 bonus to your Constitution score (to a maximum of 20), advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made to interact with dwarves, darkvision out to 60 feet, the ability to speak, read, and write Dwarvish, and resistance to poison damage. Each day at dawn, there is a 50 percent chance you grow a full beard or your existing beard becomes visibly thicker.

title: Belt of Stone Giant Strength
description: While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 23. The item has no effect on you if your Strength is already 23 or higher.

title: Belt of Frost Giant Strength
description: While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 23. The item has no effect on you if your Strength is already 23 or higher.

title: Belt of Fire Giant Strength
description: While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 25. The item has no effect on you if your Strength is already 25 or higher.

title: Belt of Cloud Giant Strength
description: While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 27. The item has no effect on you if your Strength is already 27 or higher.

title: Belt of Storm Giant Strength
description: While wearing this belt, your Strength score changes to 29. The item has no effect on you if your Strength is already 29 or higher.

title: Boots of Levitation
description: While you wear these boots, you can use an action to cast the levitate spell on yourself at will.

title: Boots of Speed
description: While you wear these boots, you can use a bonus action to click the boots' heels together. The boots double your walking speed for up to 10 minutes, and any creature that makes an opportunity attack against you has disadvantage on the attack roll. The effect ends early if you click your heels together again. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Boots of Striding and Springing
description: While you wear these boots, your walking speed becomes 30 feet, unless your walking speed is higher, and your speed isn't reduced if you are encumbered or wearing heavy armor. In addition, you can jump three times the normal distance, though you can't jump farther than your remaining movement would allow.

title: Boots of the Winterlands
description: These furred boots are snug and feel quite warm. While you wear them, you gain resistance to cold damage and you ignore difficult terrain created by ice or snow. You can tolerate temperatures as low as -50 degrees Fahrenheit without any additional protection.

title: Boots of False Tracks
description: Only humanoids can wear these boots. While wearing the boots, you can choose to have them leave tracks like those of another kind of humanoid of your size.

title: Slippers of Spider Climbing
description: While you wear these light shoes, you can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving your hands free. You have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. However, the slippers don't allow you to move this way on a slippery surface, such as one covered by ice or oil.

title: Winged Boots
description: While you wear these boots, you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed. You can use the boots to fly for up to 4 hours, all at once or in several shorter flights, each one using a minimum of 1 minute from the duration. If you are flying when the duration expires, you descend at a rate of 30 feet per round until you land. The boots regain 2 hours of flying capability for every 12 hours they aren't in use.

title: Bracers of Archery
description: While wearing these bracers, you have proficiency with the longbow and shortbow, and you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls on ranged attacks made with such weapons.

title: Bracers of Defense
description: While wearing these bracers, you gain a +2 bonus to AC if you are wearing no armor and using no shield.

title: Wings of Flying
description: While wearing this cloak, you can use an action to speak its command word, turning the cloak into a pair of bat wings or bird wings on your back for 1 hour or until you repeat the command word. The wings give you a flying speed of 60 feet. When they disappear, you can't use them again for 1d12 hours.

title: Cloak of Arachnida
description: This fine garment is made of black silk interwoven with faint silvery threads. While wearing it, you gain resistance to poison damage, have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, can move across and climb web surfaces freely, can't be caught in webs of any sort, and can cast the web spell (save DC 13) once per long rest.

title: Cloak of Elvenkind
description: While you wear this cloak with its hood up, Wisdom (Perception) checks made to see you have disadvantage, and you have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide, as the cloak's color shifts to camouflage you. Pulling the hood up or down requires an action.

title: Cloak of Invisibility
description: While wearing this cloak, you can pull its hood over your head to cause yourself to become invisible. While you are invisible, anything you are carrying or wearing is invisible with you. You become visible when you cease wearing the hood. Pulling the hood up or down requires an action. The cloak can be used for a total of 2 hours, after which it loses its magic. Deducting time in increments of 1 minute, the cloak regains 1 hour of duration for every 12 hours not in use.

title: Cloak of the Bat
description: While wearing this cloak, you have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. In an area of dim light or darkness, you can grip the edges of the cloak with both hands and use it to fly at a speed of 40 feet. If you ever fail to grip the cloak's edges while flying, or if you are no longer in dim light or darkness, you lose this flying speed. You can also use an action to polymorph into a bat as if you had cast the polymorph spell on yourself.

title: Cloak of the Manta Ray
description: While wearing this cloak with its hood up, you can breathe underwater, and you have a swimming speed of 60 feet. Pulling the hood up or down requires an action.

title: Cloak of Billowing
description: While wearing this cloak, you can use a bonus action to make it billow dramatically.

title: Cloak of Many Fashions
description: While wearing this cloak, you can use a bonus action to change the style, color, and apparent quality of the garment. The cloak's weight doesn't change. Regardless of its appearance, the cloak can't be anything but a cloak.

title: Mantle of Spell Resistance
description: You have advantage on saving throws against spells while you wear this cloak.

title: Nature's Mantle
description: This cloak shifts color and texture to blend with the terrain surrounding you. While wearing the cloak, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your druid or ranger spells. While you are in an area that is lightly obscured, you can Hide as a bonus action even if you are being directly observed.

title: Gloves of Missile Snaring
description: These gloves seem to almost meld into your hands when you don them. When a ranged weapon attack hits you while you're wearing them, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage by 1d10 + your Dexterity modifier, provided you have a free hand. If you reduce the damage to 0, you can catch the missile if it is small enough for you to hold in that hand.

title: Gloves of Swimming and Climbing
description: While wearing these gloves, climbing and swimming don't cost you extra movement, and you gain a +5 bonus to Strength (Athletics) checks made to climb or swim.

title: Gloves of Thievery
description: These gloves are invisible while worn. While wearing them, you gain a +5 bonus to Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) checks and Dexterity checks made to pick locks.

title: Robe of Eyes
description: This robe is adorned with eyelike patterns. While you wear the robe, you gain darkvision out to 120 feet, can see invisible creatures and objects, and can see into the Ethereal Plane out to 120 feet. The eyes on the robe can't be closed or averted, making you vulnerable to the blinded condition from effects like the light or daylight spell if you fail a Constitution saving throw.

title: Robe of Scintillating Colors
description: This robe has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While you wear it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to cause the garment to display a shifting pattern of dazzling hues. During this time, the robe sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Each creature within 30 feet that can see you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or become stunned until the effect ends.

title: Robe of Stars
description: This black or dark blue robe is embroidered with small white or silver stars and grants you a +1 bonus to saving throws while you wear it. The robe has 6 stars that can be removed and thrown as magic missile darts (5th-level), and you can use an action to enter the Astral Plane along with everything you are wearing and carrying. The robe regains 1d6 removed stars daily at dawn.

title: Robe of the Archmagi
attunement: by a Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
description: This elegant garment is made from exquisite cloth and set with numerous tiny stars. While wearing this robe, your base AC is 15 + your Dexterity modifier if you aren't wearing armor. You also gain advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects, and your spell save DC and spell attack bonus each increase by 2.

title: Robe of Useful Items
description: This robe has cloth patches of various shapes and colors covering it. While wearing the robe, you can use an action to detach one of the patches, causing it to become the object or creature it represents. The robe comes with two each of dagger, bullseye lantern, steel mirror, 10-foot pole, hempen rope, and sack patches, along with 4d4 additional random patches determined by rolling on the robe's table.

title: Weapon, +2
description: You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

title: Weapon, +3
description: You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

title: Vicious Weapon
description: When you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this magic weapon, the target takes an extra 7 damage of the weapon's type.

title: Berserker Axe
description: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, while you are attuned to this weapon, your hit point maximum increases by 1 for each level you have attained. When you are damaged by a hostile creature, you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or go berserk, using your action each round to attack the nearest creature with this weapon until no creatures are within 60 feet that you can see or hear.

title: Giant Slayer
description: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a Giant with it, the giant takes an extra 2d6 damage of the weapon's type and must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or fall prone.

title: Club
description: A simple bludgeoning weapon. Deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage. Properties: light. Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 1 sp.

title: Dagger
description: A small blade favored by rogues and spellcasters as a sidearm. Deals 1d4 piercing damage. Properties: finesse, light, thrown (range 20/60). Weight: 1 lb. Cost: 2 gp.

title: Greatclub
description: A heavy, crude bludgeoning weapon requiring both hands to wield. Deals 1d8 bludgeoning damage. Properties: two-handed. Weight: 10 lb. Cost: 2 sp.

title: Handaxe
description: A small, light axe suitable for melee combat or throwing. Deals 1d6 slashing damage. Properties: light, thrown (range 20/60). Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 5 gp.

title: Javelin
description: A light spear designed for throwing. Deals 1d6 piercing damage. Properties: thrown (range 30/120). Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 5 sp.

title: Light Hammer
description: A small hammer that can be used in melee or thrown at a target. Deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage. Properties: light, thrown (range 20/60). Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 2 gp.

title: Mace
description: A simple bludgeoning weapon consisting of a heavy head on a solid handle. Deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage. Weight: 4 lb. Cost: 5 gp.

title: Quarterstaff
description: A versatile wooden staff used by monks and travelers alike. Deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage. Properties: versatile (1d8). Weight: 4 lb. Cost: 2 sp.

title: Sickle
description: A curved blade originally designed for harvesting grain, also used as a light weapon. Deals 1d4 slashing damage. Properties: light. Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 1 gp.

title: Spear
description: A versatile polearm that can be wielded in one or two hands, or thrown at a target. Deals 1d6 piercing damage. Properties: thrown (range 20/60), versatile (1d8). Weight: 3 lb. Cost: 1 gp.

title: Dart
description: A small, pointed missile weapon designed for throwing. Deals 1d4 piercing damage. Properties: finesse, thrown (range 20/60). Weight: 1/4 lb. Cost: 5 cp.

title: Light Crossbow
description: A mechanical ranged weapon that uses bolts as ammunition. Deals 1d8 piercing damage. Properties: ammunition (range 80/320), loading, two-handed. Weight: 5 lb. Cost: 25 gp.

title: Shortbow
description: A small bow favored for its portability. Deals 1d6 piercing damage. Properties: ammunition (range 80/320), two-handed. Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 25 gp.

title: Sling
description: A simple ranged weapon that hurls stones or bullets at a target. Deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage. Properties: ammunition (range 30/120). Weight: 0 lb. Cost: 1 sp.

title: Battleaxe
description: A versatile axe designed for combat, capable of being wielded in one or two hands. Deals 1d8 slashing damage. Properties: versatile (1d10). Weight: 4 lb. Cost: 10 gp.

title: Flail
description: A weapon consisting of a spiked ball attached to a handle by a chain. Deals 1d8 bludgeoning damage. Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 10 gp.

title: Glaive
description: A polearm with a single-edged blade at the end, offering superior reach. Deals 1d10 slashing damage. Properties: heavy, reach, two-handed. Weight: 6 lb. Cost: 20 gp.

title: Greataxe
description: A massive axe requiring two hands to wield, favored by barbarians. Deals 1d12 slashing damage. Properties: heavy, two-handed. Weight: 7 lb. Cost: 30 gp.

title: Greatsword
description: A large, heavy sword requiring two hands to wield effectively. Deals 2d6 slashing damage. Properties: heavy, two-handed. Weight: 6 lb. Cost: 50 gp.

title: Halberd
description: A polearm combining an axe blade with a spike, offering both reach and versatility. Deals 1d10 slashing damage. Properties: heavy, reach, two-handed. Weight: 6 lb. Cost: 10 gp.

title: Lance
description: A long weapon designed for use while mounted. Deals 1d12 piercing damage. Properties: reach, special (you have disadvantage when you use a lance to attack a target within 5 feet of you, and a lance requires two hands to wield when you aren't mounted). Weight: 6 lb. Cost: 10 gp.

title: Longsword
description: A versatile sword that can be wielded in one or two hands. Deals 1d8 slashing damage. Properties: versatile (1d10). Weight: 3 lb. Cost: 15 gp.

title: Maul
description: A massive two-handed hammer capable of devastating blows. Deals 2d6 bludgeoning damage. Properties: heavy, two-handed. Weight: 10 lb. Cost: 10 gp.

title: Morningstar
description: A spiked mace that delivers piercing blows. Deals 1d8 piercing damage. Weight: 4 lb. Cost: 15 gp.

title: Pike
description: A very long thrusting polearm used to keep enemies at bay. Deals 1d10 piercing damage. Properties: heavy, reach, two-handed. Weight: 18 lb. Cost: 5 gp.

title: Rapier
description: A slender, sharply pointed sword designed for thrusting attacks. Deals 1d8 piercing damage. Properties: finesse. Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 25 gp.

title: Scimitar
description: A curved, single-edged blade favored by dervishes and sailors. Deals 1d6 slashing damage. Properties: finesse, light. Weight: 3 lb. Cost: 25 gp.

title: Shortsword
description: A light, short blade ideal for dual wielding. Deals 1d6 piercing damage. Properties: finesse, light. Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 10 gp.

title: Trident
description: A three-pronged spear that can be thrown or wielded in melee. Deals 1d6 piercing damage. Properties: thrown (range 20/60), versatile (1d8). Weight: 4 lb. Cost: 5 gp.

title: War Pick
description: A military pick designed to punch through armor. Deals 1d8 piercing damage. Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 5 gp.

title: Warhammer
description: A versatile hammer designed for battle, popular among dwarves and clerics. Deals 1d8 bludgeoning damage. Properties: versatile (1d10). Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 15 gp.

title: Whip
description: A braided leather weapon that can strike at a distance. Deals 1d4 slashing damage. Properties: finesse, reach. Weight: 3 lb. Cost: 2 gp.

title: Blowgun
description: A tube through which darts are blown at a target, often used with poisoned needles. Deals 1 piercing damage. Properties: ammunition (range 25/100), loading. Weight: 1 lb. Cost: 10 gp.

title: Hand Crossbow
description: A small crossbow that can be wielded in one hand, favored by rogues. Deals 1d6 piercing damage. Properties: ammunition (range 30/120), light, loading. Weight: 3 lb. Cost: 75 gp.

title: Heavy Crossbow
description: A large, powerful crossbow that fires heavy bolts with great force. Deals 1d10 piercing damage. Properties: ammunition (range 100/400), heavy, loading, two-handed. Weight: 18 lb. Cost: 50 gp.

title: Longbow
description: A tall bow with impressive range, requiring significant strength to draw. Deals 1d8 piercing damage. Properties: ammunition (range 150/600), heavy, two-handed. Weight: 2 lb. Cost: 50 gp.

title: Net
description: A mesh of rope used to entangle creatures. A Large or smaller creature hit by a net is restrained until it is freed. A creature can use its action to make a DC 10 Strength check to free itself or another creature. Properties: special, thrown (range 5/15). Weight: 3 lb. Cost: 1 gp.

title: Dancing Sword
description: You can use a bonus action to toss this magic sword into the air and speak the command word. When you do so, the sword begins to hover, flies up to 30 feet, and attacks one creature of your choice within 5 feet of it using your attack bonus and ability modifier for damage. On each of your turns, you can use a bonus action to cause the sword to fly up to 30 feet and repeat the attack against a target within 5 feet of it. After the sword attacks for the fourth time, it flies up to 30 feet and tries to return to your hand.

title: Defender
description: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The first time you attack with the sword on each of your turns, you can transfer some or all of the sword's bonus to your Armor Class instead of using the bonus on any attacks that turn. The adjusted bonuses remain in effect until the start of your next turn.

title: Dragon Slayer
description: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a dragon with this weapon, the dragon takes an extra 3d6 damage of the weapon's type.

title: Frost Brand
description: When you hit with an attack using this magic sword, the target takes an extra 1d6 cold damage. In addition, while you hold the sword, you have resistance to fire damage. In freezing temperatures, the blade sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. When you draw this weapon, you can extinguish all nonmagical flames within 30 feet of you.

title: Holy Avenger
description: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a fiend or an undead with it, that creature takes an extra 2d10 radiant damage. While you hold the drawn sword, it creates an aura in a 10-foot radius around you that grants you and all friendly creatures in the aura advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

title: Luck Blade
description: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While the sword is on your person, you also gain a +1 bonus to saving throws. The sword has 1d4 - 1 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to cast the wish spell from it. Once all charges are expended, the sword loses this property.

title: Moon-Touched Sword
description: In darkness, the unsheathed blade of this sword sheds moonlight, creating bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet.

title: Nine Lives Stealer
description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The sword has 1d8 + 1 charges. If you score a critical hit against a creature that has fewer than 100 hit points, it must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be slain instantly as the sword tears its life force from its body. A construct or an undead is immune to this effect. The sword loses 1 charge each time it slays a creature.

title: Sword of Answering
attunement: by a creature with a matching alignment
description: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, while you hold the sword, you can use your reaction to make one melee attack with it against any creature in your reach that deals damage to you. You have advantage on the attack roll.

title: Sword of Life Stealing
description: When you attack a creature with this magic weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, that target takes an extra 10 necrotic damage if it isn't a construct or an undead. You also gain 10 temporary hit points.

title: Sword of Sharpness
item_type: Weapon (any sword that deals slashing damage)
description: When you attack an object with this magic sword and hit, maximize your weapon damage dice against the target. When you attack a creature with this weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, that target takes an extra 14 slashing damage and you roll another d20. If you roll a 20, you lop off one of the target's limbs. The sword also sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet when you speak its command word.

title: Sword of Wounding
description: Hit points lost to this weapon's damage can be regained only through a short or long rest, rather than by regeneration, magic, or any other means. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack using this magic weapon, you can wound the target. The wounded target takes 1d4 necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns for each time you've wounded it. The target or a creature within 5 feet can use an action to make a DC 15 Wisdom (Medicine) check, ending the wound on a success.

title: Staff of Charming
attunement: by a Bard, Cleric, Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
description: While holding this staff, you can use an action to expend 1 of its 10 charges to cast charm person, command, or comprehend languages from it using your spell save DC. The staff regains 1d8 + 2 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20; on a 1, the staff becomes a nonmagical quarterstaff. If you succeed on a save against an enchantment spell targeting only you while holding the staff, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge to turn the spell back on its caster.

title: Staff of Fire
attunement: by a Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
description: You have resistance to fire damage while you hold this staff. The staff has 10 charges and regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20; on a 1, the staff blackens and crumbles to ash. While holding it, you can use an action to expend charges to cast burning hands (1 charge), fireball (3 charges), or wall of fire (4 charges) from it, using your spell save DC.

title: Staff of Frost
attunement: by a Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
description: You have resistance to cold damage while you hold this staff. The staff has 10 charges and regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20; on a 1, the staff turns to water and is destroyed. While holding it, you can use an action to expend charges to cast cone of cold (5 charges), fog cloud (1 charge), ice storm (4 charges), or wall of ice (4 charges) from it, using your spell save DC.

title: Staff of Healing
attunement: by a Bard, Cleric, or Druid
description: This staff has 10 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC and spellcasting ability modifier: cure wounds (1 charge per spell level, up to 4th), lesser restoration (2 charges), or mass cure wounds (5 charges). The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20; on a 1, the staff vanishes in a flash of light.

title: Staff of Striking
description: This staff has 10 charges. When you hit with a melee attack using it, you can expend up to 3 of its charges. For each charge you expend, the target takes an extra 1d6 force damage. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20; on a 1, the staff becomes a nonmagical quarterstaff. This staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

title: Staff of Swarming Insects
attunement: by a Bard, Cleric, Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
description: This staff has 10 charges and regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20; on a 1, a swarm of insects consumes the staff and destroys it. While holding the staff, you can use an action to expend charges to cast giant insect (4 charges) or insect plague (5 charges) from it, using your spell save DC. You can also use an action to expend 1 charge to cause a swarm of harmless flying insects to spread out in a 30-foot radius from you, creating heavily obscured area for 10 minutes.

title: Staff of the Magi
attunement: by a Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
description: This staff has 50 charges and can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls and a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls. While holding it, you have advantage on saving throws against spells, and you can use an action to expend charges to cast a wide variety of spells. When the last charge is expended, you can break the staff in a retributive strike, creating a devastating explosion. The staff regains 4d6 + 2 expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Staff of the Python
attunement: by a Cleric, Druid, or Warlock
description: You can use an action to speak this staff's command word and throw it on the ground within 10 feet of you. The staff becomes a giant constrictor snake under your control. The snake acts on your turn and reverts to staff form when it drops to 0 hit points, when you use a bonus action to revert it, or when you die. If the snake is reduced to 0 hit points, the staff is destroyed.

title: Staff of the Woodlands
description: This staff has 10 charges and can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls. While holding it, you have a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. You can use an action to expend charges to cast animal friendship (1 charge), awaken (5 charges), barkskin (2 charges), locate animals or plants (2 charges), speak with animals (1 charge), speak with plants (3 charges), or wall of thorns (6 charges). You can also use an action to plant the staff in fertile earth and expend 1 charge to transform it into a 60-foot tall tree.

title: Staff of Thunder and Lightning
description: This staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls. It also has several special abilities that can each be used once per day: Lightning (5d6 lightning damage on a hit), Thunder (DC 17 Constitution save or stunned for 1 turn on a hit), Lightning Strike (8d6 lightning bolt), Thunderclap (DC 17 Constitution save or deafened/stunned in a 60-foot radius), and Thunder and Lightning (combines Lightning and Thunder on a single hit).

title: Staff of Withering
attunement: by a Cleric, Druid, or Warlock
description: This staff has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. The staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff. On a hit, you can expend 1 charge to deal an extra 2d10 necrotic damage to the target, and the target must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage for 1 hour on any ability check or saving throw that uses Strength or Constitution.

title: Staff of Adornment
description: If you place an object weighing no more than 1 pound above the tip of the staff while holding it, the object floats an inch from the staff's tip and remains there until it is removed or until the staff is no longer in your possession. The staff can have up to three such objects floating over its tip at any given time.

title: Staff of Birdcalls
description: This wooden staff is decorated with bird carvings. It has 10 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to create one of the following sounds: a finch's chirp, a raven's caw, a loon's call, a goose's honk, an owl's hoot, or a random bird sound. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20; on a 1, the staff explodes in a harmless cloud of feathers.

title: Staff of Flowers
description: This wooden staff has 10 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to cause a flower to sprout from a patch of earth or soil within 5 feet of you, or from the staff itself. The flower is a choice of a chrysanthemum, daffodil, lily, lotus, rose, sunflower, or tulip, and is harmless and nonmagical. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20; on a 1, the staff turns into flower petals and is lost.

title: Mace of Disruption
description: When you hit a fiend or an undead with this magic weapon, that creature takes an extra 2d6 radiant damage. If the target has 25 hit points or fewer after taking this damage, it must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be destroyed. On a successful save, the creature becomes frightened of you until the end of your next turn. While you hold this weapon, it sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

title: Mace of Smiting
description: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The bonus increases to +3 when you use the mace to attack a construct. When you roll a 20 on an attack roll made with this weapon, the target takes an extra 7 bludgeoning damage, or an extra 14 bludgeoning damage if it's a construct. If a construct has 25 hit points or fewer after taking this damage, it is destroyed.

title: Mace of Terror
description: This magic weapon has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to release a wave of terror. Each creature of your choice in a 30-foot radius extending from you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened of you for 1 minute. While frightened, a creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as possible. The frightened creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns.

title: Moon Sickle, +1
description: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While holding this silver-bladed sickle under moonlight, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your druid and ranger spells. In addition, you can use the sickle as a spellcasting focus for your druid and ranger spells, and when you cast a spell that restores hit points, you can roll a d4 and add the result to the total hit points restored.

title: Moon Sickle, +2
description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While holding this silver-bladed sickle under moonlight, you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your druid and ranger spells. In addition, you can use the sickle as a spellcasting focus for your druid and ranger spells, and when you cast a spell that restores hit points, you can roll a d4 and add the result to the total hit points restored.

title: Moon Sickle, +3
description: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While holding this silver-bladed sickle under moonlight, you gain a +3 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your druid and ranger spells. In addition, you can use the sickle as a spellcasting focus for your druid and ranger spells, and when you cast a spell that restores hit points, you can roll a d4 and add the result to the total hit points restored.

title: Javelin of Lightning
description: This javelin is a magic weapon. When you hurl it and speak its command word, it transforms into a bolt of lightning, forming a line 5 feet wide that extends out from you to a target within 120 feet. Each creature in the line excluding you and the target must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking 4d6 lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The javelin then reverts to its normal form and its property can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Scimitar of Speed
description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, you can make one attack with it as a bonus action on each of your turns.

title: Oathbow
description: When you nock an arrow on this bow, it whispers in Elvish, "Swift defeat to my enemies." When you use this weapon to make a ranged attack and say the command phrase "Swift death to you who have wronged me," the target of that attack becomes your sworn enemy until it dies or until dawn seven days later. You have advantage on attack rolls against your sworn enemy, your attacks deal an extra 3d6 piercing damage against it, and it gains no benefit from cover other than total cover. You can have only one sworn enemy at a time, and you have disadvantage on attack rolls with all other weapons while your sworn enemy lives.

title: Sun Blade
description: This item appears to be a longsword hilt. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to cause a blade of pure radiance to spring into existence or disappear. While the blade exists, this magic longsword has the finesse property. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon, which deals radiant damage instead of slashing damage. When you hit an undead with it, that target takes an extra 1d8 radiant damage. The sword's luminous blade emits bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet, and you can change the radius as an action.

title: Dwarven Thrower
description: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. It has the thrown property with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. When you hit with a ranged attack using this weapon, it deals an extra 1d8 damage or, if the target is a giant, 2d8 damage. Immediately after the attack, the weapon flies back to your hand.

title: Hammer of Thunderbolts
description: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While you wear a belt of giant strength and gauntlets of ogre power, the bonus increases to +4, you gain immunity to giant-thrown boulder attacks, and when you hit a giant with this weapon, it must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or die. You can also use the weapon to hurl a thunderbolt at a point within 120 feet, requiring each creature within 30 feet to make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking 5d6 thunder and 5d6 lightning damage on a failure.

title: Trident of Fish Command
description: This trident is a magic weapon. It has 3 charges. While you carry it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to cast dominate beast (save DC 15) from it on a beast that has an innate swimming speed. The trident regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Dagger of Venom
description: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You can use an action to cause thick, black poison to coat the blade, which lasts for 1 minute or until an attack using this weapon hits a creature. That creature must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or take 2d10 poison damage and become poisoned for 1 minute. The dagger can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

title: Immovable Rod
description: This flat iron rod has a button on one end. You can use an action to press the button, which causes the rod to become magically fixed in place. Until you or another creature uses an action to push the button again, the rod doesn't move, even if it is defying gravity. The rod can hold up to 8,000 pounds of weight; more weight causes the rod to deactivate and fall.

title: Rod of Absorption
description: While holding this rod, you can use your reaction to absorb a spell that is targeting only you and not with an area of effect. The absorbed spell's effect is canceled, and the spell's energy is stored in the rod. The energy has the same level as the spell when it was cast, and you can use the stored levels to cast spells you have prepared.

title: Rod of Alertness
description: This rod has a flanged head and the following properties. While holding the rod, you have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks and on rolls for initiative. The rod has various additional properties including the ability to cast certain spells and plant it in the ground to create a protective ward.

title: Rod of Lordly Might
description: This rod has a flanged head, and it functions as a magic mace that grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. The rod has six buttons along its haft, each of which causes the rod to transform in a different way, such as becoming a flame tongue longsword, a battleaxe, or a battering ram.

title: Rod of Rulership
description: You can use an action to present the rod and command obedience from each creature of your choice that you can see within 120 feet of you. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for 8 hours. A charmed target regards you as its trusted leader. The rod can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Rod of Security
description: While holding this rod, you can use an action to activate it, transporting you and up to 199 other willing creatures you can see to a paradise that exists in an extraplanar space. You choose the form the paradise takes, and creatures regain hit points while resting there. The rod can't be used again for 10 days.

title: Rod of the Pact Keeper, +1
description: While holding this rod, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your warlock spells. In addition, you can regain one warlock spell slot as an action while holding the rod. You can't use this property again until you finish a long rest.

title: Rod of the Pact Keeper, +2
description: While holding this rod, you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your warlock spells. In addition, you can regain one warlock spell slot as an action while holding the rod. You can't use this property again until you finish a long rest.

title: Rod of the Pact Keeper, +3
description: While holding this rod, you gain a +3 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your warlock spells. In addition, you can regain one warlock spell slot as an action while holding the rod. You can't use this property again until you finish a long rest.

title: Wand of Binding
description: This wand has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action to expend charges to cast the hold monster spell (5 charges) or the hold person spell (2 charges) from it, using your spell save DC.

title: Wand of Enemy Detection
description: This wand has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to speak its command word, learning the direction of the nearest creature hostile to you within 60 feet, though not its distance or identity.

title: Wand of Fear
description: This wand has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the wand, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to issue a command that causes all creatures within 60 feet to become frightened, or expend 2 charges to cast the fear spell (save DC 15).

title: Wand of Fireballs
description: This wand has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more charges to cast the fireball spell (save DC 15) from it. For 1 charge, you cast the 3rd-level version, and the spell level increases by one for each additional charge expended.

title: Wand of Lightning Bolts
description: This wand has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more charges to cast the lightning bolt spell (save DC 15) from it. For 1 charge, you cast the 3rd-level version, and the spell level increases by one for each additional charge expended.

title: Wand of Magic Detection
description: This wand has 3 charges. While holding it, you can expend 1 charge as an action to cast the detect magic spell from it. The wand regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Wand of Magic Missiles
description: This wand has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more charges to cast the magic missile spell from it. For 1 charge, you cast the 1st-level version, and the spell level increases by one for each additional charge expended.

title: Wand of Paralysis
description: This wand has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to cause a thin blue ray to streak from the tip toward a creature within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be paralyzed for 1 minute.

title: Wand of Polymorph
description: This wand has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to cast the polymorph spell (save DC 15) from it.

title: Wand of Secrets
description: The wand has 3 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge, and if a secret door or trap is within 30 feet of you, the wand pulses and points at the one nearest to you. The wand regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Wand of the War Mage, +1
description: While holding this wand, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls. In addition, you ignore half cover when making a spell attack.

title: Wand of the War Mage, +2
description: While holding this wand, you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls. In addition, you ignore half cover when making a spell attack.

title: Wand of the War Mage, +3
description: While holding this wand, you gain a +3 bonus to spell attack rolls. In addition, you ignore half cover when making a spell attack.

title: Wand of Web
description: This wand has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to cast the web spell (save DC 15) from it.

title: Wand of Wonder
description: This wand has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and choose a target within 120 feet. The target can be a creature, object, or point in space. Roll d100 and consult the table to determine the wand's random magical effect.

title: Wand of Conducting
description: This wand has 3 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to create orchestral music by waving it around. The music can be heard out to a range of 120 feet and ends when you stop waving the wand. The wand regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Wand of Pyrotechnics
description: This wand has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and create a harmless burst of multicolored light at a point you can see up to 60 feet away, accompanied by a crackling noise heard 300 feet away.

title: Wand of Scowls
description: This wand has 3 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and target a humanoid you can see within 30 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 10 Charisma saving throw or be forced to scowl for 1 minute. The wand regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Wand of Smiles
description: This wand has 3 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and target a humanoid you can see within 30 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 10 Charisma saving throw or be forced to smile for 1 minute. The wand regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Ioun Stone of Absorption
description: This pale lavender ellipsoid orbits your head while attuned. You can use your reaction to cancel a spell of 4th level or lower cast by a creature you can see targeting only you. Once the stone has canceled 20 levels of spells, it burns out and turns dull gray, losing its magic.

title: Ioun Stone of Agility
description: This deep red sphere orbits your head while attuned and increases your Dexterity score by 2, to a maximum of 20.

title: Ioun Stone of Awareness
description: This dark blue rhomboid orbits your head while attuned and grants you the ability to not be surprised. While you are conscious, you can't be surprised.

title: Ioun Stone of Fortitude
description: This pink rhomboid orbits your head while attuned and increases your Constitution score by 2, to a maximum of 20.

title: Ioun Stone of Greater Absorption
description: This marbled lavender and green ellipsoid orbits your head while attuned. You can use your reaction to cancel a spell of 8th level or lower cast by a creature you can see targeting only you. Once the stone has canceled 50 levels of spells, it burns out and turns dull gray, losing its magic.

title: Ioun Stone of Insight
description: This incandescent blue sphere orbits your head while attuned and increases your Wisdom score by 2, to a maximum of 20.

title: Ioun Stone of Intellect
description: This marbled scarlet and blue sphere orbits your head while attuned and increases your Intelligence score by 2, to a maximum of 20.

title: Ioun Stone of Leadership
description: This marbled pink and green sphere orbits your head while attuned and increases your Charisma score by 2, to a maximum of 20.

title: Ioun Stone of Mastery
description: This pale green prism orbits your head while attuned and increases your proficiency bonus by 1.

title: Ioun Stone of Protection
description: This dusty rose prism orbits your head while attuned and grants you a +1 bonus to AC.

title: Ioun Stone of Regeneration
description: This pearly white spindle orbits your head while attuned and allows you to regain 15 hit points at the end of each hour, provided that you have at least 1 hit point.

title: Ioun Stone of Reserve
description: This vibrant purple prism orbits your head while attuned and stores spells cast into it, holding them until you use them. The stone can store up to 3 levels worth of spells at a time. When found, it contains 1d4 - 1 levels of stored spells chosen by the DM.

title: Ioun Stone of Strength
description: This pale blue rhomboid orbits your head while attuned and increases your Strength score by 2, to a maximum of 20.

title: Ioun Stone of Sustenance
description: This clear spindle orbits your head while attuned and allows you to go without food and water. You don't need to eat or drink while this ioun stone orbits your head.

title: Alchemy Jug
description: This ceramic jug appears to be able to hold a gallon of liquid and weighs 12 pounds whether full or empty. You can use an action and name one liquid from the jug's table to cause the jug to produce the chosen liquid. The jug can produce a variety of liquids including acid, basic poison, beer, honey, mayonnaise, oil, vinegar, water, and wine.

title: All-Purpose Tool, +1
description: This simple screwdriver can transform into a variety of artisan's tools. While holding this tool, you gain a +1 bonus to the saving throw DCs of your artificer spells. As an action, you can transform it into any type of artisan's tool, and you can also use it as a spellcasting focus for your artificer spells.

title: All-Purpose Tool, +2
description: This simple screwdriver can transform into a variety of artisan's tools. While holding this tool, you gain a +2 bonus to the saving throw DCs of your artificer spells. As an action, you can transform it into any type of artisan's tool, and you can also use it as a spellcasting focus for your artificer spells.

title: All-Purpose Tool, +3
description: This simple screwdriver can transform into a variety of artisan's tools. While holding this tool, you gain a +3 bonus to the saving throw DCs of your artificer spells. As an action, you can transform it into any type of artisan's tool, and you can also use it as a spellcasting focus for your artificer spells.

title: Apparatus of Kwalish
description: This item first appears to be a Large sealed iron barrel weighing 500 pounds. It has a hidden catch that can be found with a DC 20 Intelligence (Investigation) check, opening to reveal a set of levers that allow it to unfold into a mechanical lobster-shaped vehicle that can walk and swim, holding up to two Medium creatures.

title: Arcane Grimoire, +1
description: While you are holding this leather-bound book, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells, and you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your wizard spells. You can also use this book as your spellbook. When you use your Arcane Recovery feature, you can increase the number of spell slot levels you regain by 1.

title: Arcane Grimoire, +2
description: While you are holding this leather-bound book, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells, and you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your wizard spells. You can also use this book as your spellbook. When you use your Arcane Recovery feature, you can increase the number of spell slot levels you regain by 1.

title: Arcane Grimoire, +3
description: While you are holding this leather-bound book, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells, and you gain a +3 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your wizard spells. You can also use this book as your spellbook. When you use your Arcane Recovery feature, you can increase the number of spell slot levels you regain by 1.

title: Bag of Beans
description: Inside this heavy cloth bag are 3d4 dry beans. When you dump the bag's contents on the ground, they explode in a 10-foot radius dealing 5d4 fire damage. If you plant a bean in dirt or sand and then water it, a random magical effect occurs 1 minute later from the spot where it was planted.

title: Bag of Devouring
description: This bag superficially resembles a bag of holding but is a feeding orifice for a gigantic extradimensional creature. Turning the bag inside out closes the orifice. Any animal or vegetable matter placed completely inside the bag is devoured and lost forever. A creature partially inside the bag can use its action to make a DC 15 Strength check to escape.

title: Bag of Tricks
description: This ordinary bag, made from gray, rust, or tan cloth, appears empty. Reaching inside the bag reveals a small, fuzzy object. You can use an action to pull the fuzzy object from the bag and throw it up to 20 feet, where it transforms into a random creature determined by rolling on the bag's table. The creature is friendly to you and vanishes at the next dawn.

title: Bead of Force
description: This small black sphere measures 3/4 of an inch in diameter and weighs an ounce. You can use an action to throw the bead up to 60 feet, where it explodes on impact dealing 5d4 force damage in a 10-foot radius. A sphere of transparent force then encloses the area for 1 minute.

title: Bloodwell Vial, +1
description: This glass vial contains a drop of blood and is attached to a necklace. While wearing it, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your sorcerer spells. In addition, when you roll any Hit Dice to recover hit points while carrying this vial, you can regain 5 sorcery points.

title: Bloodwell Vial, +2
description: This glass vial contains a drop of blood and is attached to a necklace. While wearing it, you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your sorcerer spells. In addition, when you roll any Hit Dice to recover hit points while carrying this vial, you can regain 5 sorcery points.

title: Bloodwell Vial, +3
description: This glass vial contains a drop of blood and is attached to a necklace. While wearing it, you gain a +3 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your sorcerer spells. In addition, when you roll any Hit Dice to recover hit points while carrying this vial, you can regain 5 sorcery points.

title: Bowl of Commanding Water Elementals
description: While this bowl is filled with water, you can use an action to speak the bowl's command word and summon a water elemental, as if you had cast the conjure elemental spell. The bowl can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

title: Brazier of Commanding Fire Elementals
description: While a fire burns in this brass brazier, you can use an action to speak the brazier's command word and summon a fire elemental, as if you had cast the conjure elemental spell. The brazier can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

title: Candle of Invocation
description: This slender taper is dedicated to a deity and shares that deity's alignment. Lighting the candle sheds dim light in a 30-foot radius for 4 hours.
