While lit, creatures within its light that share its alignment have advantage on attack rolls, saving throws, and ability checks. The candle can also be used to cast the gate spell.

title: Carpet of Flying
description: You can speak the carpet's command word as an action to make it hover and fly. It moves according to your spoken directions, provided that you are within 30 feet of it. A carpet of flying comes in one of four sizes, each with a different carrying capacity and flying speed.

title: Cauldron of Rebirth
description: This Tiny cauldron bears engravings of various creatures. You can use it as a spellcasting focus and it has 3 charges, regaining 1d3 charges daily at dawn. You can expend a charge to cast the revivify spell. You can also fill the cauldron with water and perform a 1-hour ritual to create a potion of greater healing.

title: Censer of Controlling Air Elementals
description: While incense is burning in this censer, you can use an action to speak the censer's command word and summon an air elemental, as if you had cast the conjure elemental spell. The censer can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

title: Chime of Opening
description: This hollow metal tube measures about 1 foot long and weighs 1 pound. You can strike it as an action, pointing it at an object within 120 feet that can be opened, such as a door, lid, or lock. The chime issues a clear tone, and one lock or latch on the object opens unless the sound can't reach the object. The chime can be used ten times before cracking.

title: Crystal Ball
description: This crystal ball is about 6 inches in diameter. While touching it, you can cast the scrying spell (save DC 17) with it. Some crystal ball variants have additional properties, such as the crystal ball of mind reading, crystal ball of telepathy, or crystal ball of true seeing.

title: Cube of Force
description: This cube is about an inch across and has 36 charges. You can use an action to press one of the cube's six faces, expending charges to create a barrier of force centered on you. Each face creates a different wall effect that blocks specific types of matter or energy. The cube regains 1d36 expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Cubic Gate
description: This cube is 3 inches across and radiates palpable magical energy. Each of the six sides is keyed to a different plane of existence. You can use an action to press one side of the cube to cast the gate spell, opening a portal to the plane keyed to that side, or use two charges to cast the plane shift spell.

title: Daern's Instant Fortress
description: You can use an action to place this 1-inch metal cube on the ground and speak its command word, causing it to rapidly grow into a fortress. The tower is 20 feet square and 30 feet high, with arrow slits on all sides and a battlement atop it. Creatures in the area when it appears must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 10d10 bludgeoning damage.

title: Decanter of Endless Water
description: You can use an action to remove the stopper and speak one of three command words, causing fresh or salt water to pour out in varying amounts. "Stream" produces 1 gallon per round, "Fountain" produces 5 gallons per round, and "Geyser" produces 30 gallons per round in a 30-foot-long geyser.

title: Deck of Illusions
description: This box contains a set of parchment cards. A full deck has 34 cards. When you use an action to draw a card at random and throw it on the ground, an illusion of one or more creatures forms over the thrown card and remains until dispelled. The apparent creature is determined by the card drawn.

title: Deck of Many Things
description: Usually found in a box or pouch, this deck contains a number of cards made of ivory or vellum. Before you draw, you must declare how many cards you intend to draw and then draw them randomly. Any cards drawn in excess of this number have no effect. Each card's magic takes effect immediately, with dramatic and often permanent consequences.

title: Devotee's Censer
description: This censer can be wielded as a flail that deals an extra 1d8 radiant damage on a hit. When you use it as a holy symbol to cast a spell that restores hit points, the target of the spell regains 1d4 additional hit points. As a bonus action, you can speak the censer's command word to fill it with holy incense, creating a 10-foot radius of healing smoke.

title: Dimensional Shackles
description: You can use an action to place these shackles on an incapacitated creature. The shackles adjust to fit any Small to Large creature and prevent the target from using any method of extradimensional movement, including teleportation or travel to a different plane. A creature restrained by the shackles can make a DC 30 Strength (Athletics) check to break free.

title: Driftglobe
description: This small sphere of thick glass weighs 1 pound. If you are within 60 feet of it, you can speak its command word and cause it to emanate the light or daylight spell. Once the daylight spell has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn. The globe hovers in place and follows you at a distance of 5 feet.

title: Dust of Disappearance
description: Found in a small packet, this powder resembles very fine sand. When you use an action to throw the dust into the air, you and each creature and object within 10 feet of you become invisible for 2d4 minutes. The duration is the same for all subjects, and the dust is consumed when used.

title: Dust of Dryness
description: This small packet contains 1d6 + 4 pinches of dust. You can use an action to sprinkle a pinch over water, transforming a 15-foot cube of water into a marble-sized pellet. The pellet can be smashed against a hard surface to release the water. If a pinch of dust is sprinkled on a water elemental, it must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take 10d6 necrotic damage.

title: Dust of Sneezing and Choking
description: Found in a small container, this powder resembles very fine sand. It appears to be dust of disappearance, and an identify spell confirms this. When you use an action to throw a handful into the air, you and each creature that needs to breathe within 30 feet must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become unable to breathe while sneezing uncontrollably.

title: Ear Horn of Hearing
description: While held up to your ear, this horn suppresses the effects of the deafened condition on you, allowing you to hear normally.

title: Efreeti Bottle
description: When you use an action to remove the stopper of this painted brass bottle, a cloud of thick smoke flows out. At the end of your turn, the smoke disappears with a flash of harmless fire, and an efreeti appears. The DM rolls to determine the efreeti's attitude and the result, which can range from the efreeti attacking you to serving you for a period of time.

title: Elemental Gem
description: This gem contains a mote of elemental energy. When you use an action to break the gem, an elemental is summoned as if you had cast the conjure elemental spell, and the gem's magic is lost. The type of gem determines the elemental summoned: blue sapphire for air, yellow diamond for earth, red corundum for fire, or emerald for water.

title: Enduring Spellbook
description: This spellbook, along with anything written on its pages, can't be damaged by fire or immersion in water. In addition, the spellbook doesn't deteriorate with age.

title: Eversmoking Bottle
description: Smoke leaks from the lead-stoppered mouth of this brass bottle, which weighs 1 pound. When you use an action to remove the stopper, a cloud of thick smoke pours out in a 60-foot radius, and the cloud's radius increases by 10 feet each minute until it reaches 120 feet. The area within the cloud is heavily obscured.

title: Feather Token
description: This tiny object looks like a feather. Different types of feather tokens exist, each with a single use. Types include anchor, bird, fan, swan boat, tree, and whip. Each token has a different magical effect when used, such as creating a large tree, a swan boat, or a spectral bird.

title: Figurine of Wondrous Power
description: A figurine of wondrous power is a statuette of a beast small enough to fit in a pocket. If you use an action to speak the command word and throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, it becomes a living creature. Varieties include the bronze griffon, ebony fly, golden lions, ivory goats, marble elephant, obsidian steed, onyx dog, serpentine owl, and silver raven.

title: Folding Boat
description: This object appears as a wooden box that measures 12 inches long, 6 inches wide, and 6 inches deep. It can be commanded to unfold into a boat 10 feet long or a ship 24 feet long. The boat can hold four Medium creatures, while the ship can hold fifteen and has a sailing speed.

title: Gem of Brightness
description: This prism has 50 charges. While you are holding it, you can use an action to speak one of three command words to cause one of three effects: emit bright light in a 30-foot radius, fire a beam of light at a creature within 60 feet that must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be blinded, or cause the gem to flare with blinding light in a 30-foot cone.

title: Gem of Seeing
description: This gem has 3 charges. As an action, you can speak the gem's command word and expend 1 charge to gain truesight out to 120 feet for 10 minutes. The gem regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Heward's Handy Haversack
description: This backpack has a central pouch and two side pouches, each of which is an extradimensional space. The central pouch can hold up to 8 cubic feet or 80 pounds of gear, while each side pouch can hold up to 20 pounds. The backpack always weighs 5 pounds regardless of its contents, and retrieving an item from it requires only a bonus action.

title: Heward's Handy Spice Pouch
description: This belt pouch appears empty and has 10 charges. While holding the pouch, you can use an action to expend 1 charge, speak the name of any nonmagical food seasoning, and remove a pinch of the desired seasoning from the pouch. The pouch regains 1d6 + 4 charges daily at dawn.

title: Horn of Blasting
description: You can use an action to speak the horn's command word and then blow the horn, which emits a thunderous blast in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the cone must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw, taking 5d6 thunder damage on a failed save or half on a success. Glass and crystal objects in the cone take 10d6 thunder damage.

title: Horn of Silent Alarm
description: This horn has 4 charges. When you use an action to blow it, one creature of your choice within 600 feet hears the horn as if it were adjacent to it. No other creature hears the sound. The horn regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.

title: Horn of Valhalla
description: You can use an action to blow this horn, summoning warrior spirits from the plane of Ysgard to fight for you. They arrive within 60 feet of you and use the statistics of a berserker. They return to Ysgard after 1 hour or when they drop to 0 hit points. The horn comes in silver, brass, bronze, and iron varieties with increasing power.

title: Horseshoes of a Zephyr
description: These iron horseshoes come in a set of four. While all four shoes are affixed to the hooves of a horse or similar creature, they allow the creature to move normally while floating 4 inches above the ground. The creature leaves no tracks and ignores difficult terrain. It can also cross or stand above liquid surfaces.

title: Horseshoes of Speed
description: These iron horseshoes come in a set of four. While all four shoes are affixed to the hooves of a horse or similar creature, they increase the creature's walking speed by 30 feet.

title: Instrument of the Bards
description: An instrument of the bards is an exquisite musical instrument that bestows magical abilities on its attuned bard. A creature that attempts to play an instrument of the bards without being attuned takes psychic damage. Each instrument allows a bard to cast certain spells using the instrument, with seven types of increasing rarity from Fochlucan to Ollamh.

title: Iron Bands of Bilarro
description: This rusty iron sphere measures 3 inches in diameter and weighs 1 pound. You can use an action to speak the command word and throw the sphere at a Huge or smaller creature within 60 feet. On a successful ranged attack, the sphere transforms into bands of iron that restrain the target until you use a bonus action to release it or the target breaks free with a DC 20 Strength check.

title: Iron Flask
description: This iron bottle has a brass stopper. You can use an action to speak the flask's command word, targeting a creature you can see within 60 feet that originated on another plane. The target must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or be trapped inside the flask. A trapped creature can be released and forced to obey your commands for 1 hour.

title: Lantern of Revealing
description: While lit, this hooded lantern burns for 6 hours on 1 pint of oil, shedding bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Invisible creatures and objects are visible as long as they are in the lantern's bright light.

title: Lock of Trickery
description: This lock appears to be an ordinary lock and comes with a single key. A creature proficient with thieves' tools can pick this lock with a successful DC 15 Dexterity check. The DC to open it with the knock spell or similar magic is 20. The lock magically hides its keyhole, which can be found with a DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check.

title: Luckstone
description: While this polished agate is on your person, you gain a +1 bonus to ability checks and saving throws.

title: Lyre of Building
description: While holding this lyre, you can cast mending as an action. You can also play the lyre as an action to create powerful vibrations that deal damage to structures or to magically protect a structure within 300 feet, granting it immunity to damage and preventing it from being destroyed.

title: Manual of Bodily Health
description: This book contains health and diet tips, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Constitution score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. The manual then loses its magic but regains it in a century.

title: Manual of Gainful Exercise
description: This book describes fitness exercises, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Strength score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. The manual then loses its magic but regains it in a century.

title: Manual of Golems
description: This tome contains information and incantations necessary to make a particular type of golem. The DM chooses the type or determines it randomly: clay, flesh, iron, or stone. To decipher and use the manual, you must be a spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots. Creating the golem takes varying amounts of time and gold depending on the type.

title: Manual of Quickness of Action
description: This book contains coordination and balance exercises, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Dexterity score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. The manual then loses its magic but regains it in a century.

title: Mirror of Life Trapping
description: When this 4-foot-tall mirror is viewed indirectly, its surface shows faint images of creatures. The mirror has up to twelve extradimensional cells that can trap creatures. Any creature other than you that sees its reflection in the mirror must succeed on a DC 15 Charisma saving throw or be trapped in one of the cells.

title: Mystery Key
description: A question mark is worked into the head of this key. The key has a 5 percent chance of unlocking any lock into which it is inserted. Once it unlocks something, the key disappears.

title: Nolzur's Marvelous Pigments
description: Typically found in 1d4 pots inside a fine wooden box with a brush, these pigments allow you to create three-dimensional objects by painting them in two dimensions. The paint flows from the brush to form the desired object as you concentrate on its image. Each pot of paint is sufficient to cover 1,000 square feet, and the painted objects become real.

title: Orb of Direction
description: While holding this orb, you can use an action to determine which way is north. This property functions only on the Material Plane.

title: Orb of Dragonkind
description: Ages past, elves and humans waged war against evil dragons and created five Orbs of Dragonkind to help them in their fight. While attuned to an orb, you can use an action to peer into the orb's depths and speak its command word, summoning a dragon of the orb's type from within 40 miles. The orb also grants powerful abilities but carries a significant curse.

title: Orb of Time
description: While holding this orb, you can use an action to determine whether it is morning, afternoon, evening, or nighttime outside. This property functions only on the Material Plane.

title: Pearl of Power
description: While this pearl is on your person, you can use an action to speak its command word and regain one expended spell slot. If the expended slot was of 4th level or higher, the new slot is 3rd level. Once you use the pearl, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Perfume of Bewitching
description: This tiny vial contains magic perfume, enough for one use. You can use an action to apply the perfume to yourself, gaining advantage on all Charisma checks directed at humanoids of challenge rating 1 or lower for the next hour. Those subjected to the perfume's effect are not combative toward you unless you threaten or harm them.

title: Pipe of Smoke Monsters
description: While smoking this pipe, you can use an action to exhale a puff of smoke that takes the form of a single creature, such as a dragon, a flumph, or a froghemoth. The form must be small enough to fit in a 1-foot cube and loses its shape after a few seconds, becoming an ordinary puff of smoke.

title: Portable Hole
description: This fine black cloth, soft as silk, is folded up to the dimensions of a handkerchief. It unfolds into a circular sheet 6 feet in diameter. You can use an action to unfold it and place it on a solid surface, creating a 10-foot-deep extradimensional hole. Placing the hole inside an extradimensional space such as a bag of holding creates a rift to the Astral Plane.

title: Pot of Awakening
description: If you plant an ordinary shrub in this 10-pound clay pot and let it grow for 30 days, the shrub magically transforms into an awakened shrub at the end of that period. When the shrub awakens, its roots break the pot, destroying it. The awakened shrub is friendly toward you and follows your verbal commands.

title: Prosthetic Limb
description: This item replaces a lost limb such as a hand, arm, foot, leg, or similar body part. While the prosthetic is attached, it functions identically to the part it replaces. You can detach or reattach it as an action, and it can't be removed against your will.

title: Quiver of Ehlonna
description: Each of the quiver's three compartments connects to an extradimensional space. The shortest compartment holds up to sixty arrows or similar objects, the midsize compartment holds up to eighteen javelins or similar objects, and the longest compartment holds up to six long objects such as bows or quarterstaves. The quiver weighs 2 pounds regardless of its contents.

title: Restorative Ointment
description: This glass jar, 3 inches in diameter, contains 1d4 + 1 doses of a thick mixture that smells faintly of aloe. As an action, one dose can be swallowed or applied to the skin to restore 2d8 + 2 hit points, and the creature ceases to be poisoned and is cured of any disease.

title: Rhythm-Maker's Drum, +1
description: While holding this drum, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your bard spells. As a bonus action, you can play the drum to regain one use of your Bardic Inspiration feature. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Rhythm-Maker's Drum, +2
description: While holding this drum, you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your bard spells. As a bonus action, you can play the drum to regain one use of your Bardic Inspiration feature. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Rhythm-Maker's Drum, +3
description: While holding this drum, you gain a +3 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your bard spells. As a bonus action, you can play the drum to regain one use of your Bardic Inspiration feature. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Rope of Climbing
description: This 60-foot length of silk rope weighs 3 pounds and can hold up to 3,000 pounds. If you hold one end of the rope and use an action to speak the command word, the rope animates and moves at your direction, climbing along surfaces and tying itself to objects. It has AC 20, 20 hit points, and repairs itself in 1 minute.

title: Rope of Entanglement
description: This rope is 30 feet long and weighs 3 pounds. If you hold one end of the rope and use an action to speak its command word, the other end darts forward to entangle a creature you can see within 20 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or become restrained. You can release the creature by speaking a second command word.

title: Rope of Mending
description: You can cut this 50-foot coil of hempen rope into any number of smaller pieces, and then use an action to speak a command word to cause the pieces to knit back together. The pieces must be in contact with each other and not otherwise in use. A rope of mending is forever shortened if a section of it is lost or destroyed.

title: Ruby of the War Mage
description: Etched with eldritch runes, this 1-inch-diameter ruby allows you to use a simple or martial weapon as a spellcasting focus for your spells. For this property to work, you must attach the ruby to the weapon by pressing the gem against it for at least 10 minutes, bonding the ruby to the weapon.

title: Sending Stones
description: Sending stones come in pairs, with each smooth stone carved to match the other so the pairing is easily recognized. While you touch one stone, you can use an action to cast the sending spell from it, targeting the bearer of the other stone. Once the sending spell is cast through the stones, they can't be used again until the next dawn.

title: Sovereign Glue
description: This viscous, milky-white substance can form a permanent adhesive bond between any two objects. It must be stored in a jar or flask that has been coated inside with oil of slipperiness. One ounce of the glue can cover a 1-foot square surface and takes 1 minute to set. Once set, the bond it creates can be broken only by the application of universal solvent or oil of etherealness.

title: Sphere of Annihilation
description: This 2-foot-diameter black sphere is a hole in the multiverse, hovering in space and stabilized by a magical field. Any matter that touches it is instantly obliterated. A creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or be sucked in and obliterated. You can control the sphere's movement with a DC 25 Arcana check.

title: Stone of Controlling Earth Elementals
description: If the stone is touching the ground, you can use an action to speak its command word and summon an earth elemental, as if you had cast the conjure elemental spell. The stone can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

title: Tome of Clear Thought
description: This book contains memory and logic exercises, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Intelligence score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. The tome then loses its magic but regains it in a century.

title: Tome of Leadership and Influence
description: This book contains guidelines for influencing and charming others, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Charisma score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. The tome then loses its magic but regains it in a century.

title: Tome of Understanding
description: This book contains intuition and insight exercises, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Wisdom score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. The tome then loses its magic but regains it in a century.

title: Universal Solvent
description: This tube holds milky liquid with a strong alcohol smell. You can use an action to pour the contents of the tube onto a surface within reach, dissolving up to 1 square foot of adhesive it touches, including sovereign glue.

title: Well of Many Worlds
description: This fine black cloth, soft as silk, is folded up to the dimensions of a handkerchief. It unfolds into a circular sheet 6 feet in diameter. You can use an action to unfold and place the well on a solid surface, where it creates a two-way portal to another world or plane of existence chosen by the DM. The portal remains open for as long as the well is left in place.

title: Wind Fan
description: While holding this fan, you can use an action to cast the gust of wind spell (save DC 13) from it. Once used, the fan shouldn't be used again until the next dawn. Each time it is used again before then, there is a cumulative 20 percent chance that the fan is destroyed and crumbles into useless, nonmagical tatters.

title: Potion of Greater Healing
description: When you drink this potion, you regain 4d4 + 4 hit points. The potion's red liquid glimmers when agitated.

title: Potion of Superior Healing
description: When you drink this potion, you regain 8d4 + 8 hit points. The potion's red liquid glimmers when agitated.

title: Potion of Supreme Healing
description: When you drink this potion, you regain 10d4 + 20 hit points. The potion's red liquid glimmers when agitated.

title: Potion of Animal Friendship
description: When you drink this potion, you can cast the Animal Friendship spell (save DC 13) for 1 hour at will. The potion's liquid is a murky green with bits of shell floating in it.

title: Potion of Clairvoyance
description: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the Clairvoyance spell. An eyeball bobs in this yellowish liquid but vanishes when the potion is opened.

title: Potion of Climbing
description: When you drink this potion, you gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed for 1 hour. During this time, you have advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks you make to climb. The potion is separated into brown, silver, and gray layers resembling bands of stone.

title: Potion of Diminution
description: When you drink this potion, you gain the "reduce" effect of the Enlarge/Reduce spell for 1d4 hours (no concentration required). The red in the potion's liquid continuously contracts to a tiny bead and then expands to color the clear liquid around it.

title: Potion of Fire Breath
description: After drinking this potion, you can use a bonus action to exhale fire at a target within 30 feet, dealing 4d6 fire damage on a failed DC 13 Dexterity save, or half on a success. The effect ends after you exhale fire three times or 1 hour has passed.

title: Potion of Flying
description: When you drink this potion, you gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed for 1 hour and can hover. If you're in the air when the potion wears off, you fall unless you have some other means of staying aloft. The potion's clear liquid floats at the top of its container and has cloudy white impurities drifting in it.

title: Potion of Gaseous Form
description: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the Gaseous Form spell for 1 hour (no concentration required) or until you end the effect as a bonus action. The potion's container seems to hold fog that moves and pours like water.

title: Potion of Growth
description: When you drink this potion, you gain the "enlarge" effect of the Enlarge/Reduce spell for 1d4 hours (no concentration required). The red in the potion's liquid continuously expands from a tiny bead to color the clear liquid around it and then contracts.

title: Potion of Heroism
description: For 1 hour after drinking this potion, you gain 10 temporary hit points and are under the effect of the Bless spell (no concentration required). The blue potion bubbles and steams as if boiling.

title: Potion of Invisibility
description: When you drink this potion, you become invisible for 1 hour. Anything you wear or carry is invisible with you. The effect ends early if you attack or cast a spell. The potion's container looks empty but feels as though it holds liquid.

title: Potion of Invulnerability
description: For 1 minute after you drink this potion, you have resistance to all damage. The potion's syrupy liquid looks like liquefied iron.

title: Potion of Longevity
description: When you drink this potion, your physical age is reduced by 1d6 + 6 years, to a minimum of 13 years. Each subsequent time you drink this potion, there is a cumulative 10 percent chance that you instead age by 1d6 + 6 years.

title: Potion of Mind Reading
description: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the Detect Thoughts spell (save DC 13). The potion's dense, purple liquid has an ovoid cloud of pink floating in it.

title: Potion of Poison
description: This concoction looks, smells, and tastes like a Potion of Healing or other beneficial potion. However, it is actually poison masked by illusion magic. If you drink it, you take 3d6 poison damage and must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned, taking 3d6 poison damage at the start of each of your turns for the duration. The poisoned condition lasts until the damage is reduced to 0.

title: Potion of Resistance
description: When you drink this potion, you gain resistance to one type of damage for 1 hour. The DM chooses the type or determines it randomly from the standard damage types.

title: Potion of Speed
description: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the Haste spell for 1 minute (no concentration required). The potion's yellow fluid is streaked with black and swirls on its own.

title: Potion of Vitality
description: When you drink this potion, it removes any exhaustion you are suffering and cures any disease or poison affecting you. For the next 24 hours, you regain the maximum number of hit points for any Hit Die you spend. The potion's crimson liquid regularly pulses with dull light, resembling a heartbeat.

title: Potion of Water Breathing
description: You can breathe underwater for 1 hour after drinking this potion. Its cloudy green fluid smells of the sea and has a jellyfish-like bubble floating in it.

title: Potion of Hill Giant Strength
description: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 21 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score. This potion's transparent liquid has a floating sliver of fingernail from a hill giant.

title: Potion of Stone Giant Strength
description: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 23 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score. This potion's transparent liquid has a floating sliver of fingernail from a stone giant.

title: Potion of Frost Giant Strength
description: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 23 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score. This potion's transparent liquid has a floating sliver of fingernail from a frost giant.

title: Potion of Fire Giant Strength
description: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 25 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score. This potion's transparent liquid has a floating sliver of fingernail from a fire giant.

title: Potion of Cloud Giant Strength
description: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 27 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score. This potion's transparent liquid has a floating sliver of fingernail from a cloud giant.

title: Potion of Storm Giant Strength
description: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 29 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score. This potion's transparent liquid has a floating sliver of fingernail from a storm giant.

title: Elixir of Health
description: When you drink this potion, it cures any disease afflicting you, and it removes the blinded, deafened, paralyzed, and poisoned conditions. The clear red liquid has tiny bubbles of light in it.

title: Oil of Etherealness
description: Beads of this cloudy gray oil form on the outside of its container and quickly evaporate. The oil can cover a Medium or smaller creature along with its equipment. Applying the oil takes 10 minutes, and the affected creature then gains the effect of the Etherealness spell for 1 hour.

title: Oil of Sharpness
description: This clear, gelatinous oil sparkles with tiny, ultrathin silver shards. The oil can coat one slashing or piercing weapon or up to 5 pieces of ammunition. Applying the oil takes 1 minute. For 1 hour, the coated item is magical and has a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

title: Oil of Slipperiness
description: This sticky black unguent is thick and heavy in the container, but it flows quickly when poured. The oil can cover a Medium or smaller creature along with its equipment, requiring 10 minutes to apply. The affected creature then gains the effect of a Freedom of Movement spell for 8 hours. Alternatively, the oil can be poured on the ground as an action, duplicating the Grease spell in a 10-foot square for 8 hours.

title: Philter of Love
description: The next time you see a creature within 10 minutes after drinking this philter, you become charmed by that creature for 1 hour. If the creature is of a species and gender you are normally attracted to, you regard it as your true love while you are charmed.

title: Basic Poison
description: You can use the poison in this vial to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or up to three pieces of ammunition. Applying the poison takes an action. A creature hit by the poisoned weapon or ammunition must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or take 1d4 poison damage. Once applied, the poison retains potency for 1 minute before drying.

title: Assassin's Blood
description: A creature subjected to this ingested poison must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 6 (1d12) poison damage and is poisoned for 24 hours. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and isn't poisoned.

title: Burnt Othur Fumes
description: A creature subjected to this inhaled poison must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take 10 (3d6) poison damage, and must repeat the saving throw at the start of each of its turns. On each successive failed save, the creature takes 3 (1d6) poison damage. After three successful saves, the poison ends.

title: Carrion Crawler Mucus
description: This contact poison must be harvested from a dead or incapacitated carrion crawler. A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 minute. The poisoned creature is paralyzed and can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

title: Drow Poison
description: This injury poison is typically made only by the drow, and only in a place far removed from sunlight. A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour. If the saving throw fails by 5 or more, the creature is also unconscious while poisoned in this way.

title: Essence of Ether
description: A creature subjected to this inhaled poison must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become poisoned for 8 hours. The poisoned creature is unconscious and wakes up if it takes damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it awake.

title: Malice
description: A creature subjected to this inhaled poison must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become poisoned for 1 hour. The poisoned creature is blinded.

title: Midnight Tears
description: A creature that ingests this poison suffers no effect until the stroke of midnight. If the poison has not been neutralized before then, the creature must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw, taking 31 (9d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

title: Oil of Taggit
description: A creature subjected to this contact poison must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or become poisoned for 24 hours. The poisoned creature is unconscious and wakes up if it takes damage.

title: Pale Tincture
description: A creature subjected to this ingested poison must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or take 3 (1d6) poison damage and become poisoned. The poisoned creature must repeat the saving throw every 24 hours, taking 3 (1d6) poison damage on a failed save. Until the poison ends, the damage can't be healed by any means. After seven successful saving throws, the effect ends.

title: Purple Worm Poison
description: This injury poison must be harvested from a dead or incapacitated purple worm. A creature subjected to this poison must make a DC 19 Constitution saving throw, taking 42 (12d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

title: Serpent Venom
description: This injury poison must be harvested from a dead or incapacitated giant poisonous snake. A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 11 Constitution saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

title: Torpor
description: A creature subjected to this ingested poison must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become poisoned for 4d6 hours. The poisoned creature is incapacitated.

title: Truth Serum
description: A creature subjected to this ingested poison must succeed on a DC 11 Constitution saving throw or become poisoned for 1 hour. The poisoned creature can't knowingly speak a lie, as if under the effect of a Zone of Truth spell.

title: Wyvern Poison
description: This injury poison must be harvested from a dead or incapacitated wyvern. A creature subjected to this poison must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw, taking 24 (7d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

title: Spell Scroll (Cantrip)
description: A spell scroll bears the words of a single cantrip, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can read the scroll and cast the spell without providing any material components. The spell save DC is 13 and the attack bonus is +5. Once the spell is cast, the scroll crumbles to dust.

title: Spell Scroll (1st Level)
description: A spell scroll bears the words of a single 1st-level spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can read the scroll and cast the spell at 1st level without providing any material components. The spell save DC is 13 and the attack bonus is +5. Once the spell is cast, the scroll crumbles to dust.

title: Spell Scroll (2nd Level)
description: A spell scroll bears the words of a single 2nd-level spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can read the scroll and cast the spell at 2nd level without providing any material components. The spell save DC is 13 and the attack bonus is +5. If the spell is not on your class's spell list, you must make a DC 12 ability check using your spellcasting ability or the scroll is wasted.

title: Spell Scroll (3rd Level)
description: A spell scroll bears the words of a single 3rd-level spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can read the scroll and cast the spell at 3rd level without providing any material components. The spell save DC is 15 and the attack bonus is +7. If the spell is not on your class's spell list, you must make a DC 13 ability check using your spellcasting ability or the scroll is wasted.

title: Spell Scroll (4th Level)
description: A spell scroll bears the words of a single 4th-level spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can read the scroll and cast the spell at 4th level without providing any material components. The spell save DC is 15 and the attack bonus is +7. If the spell is not on your class's spell list, you must make a DC 14 ability check using your spellcasting ability or the scroll is wasted.

title: Spell Scroll (5th Level)
description: A spell scroll bears the words of a single 5th-level spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can read the scroll and cast the spell at 5th level without providing any material components. The spell save DC is 17 and the attack bonus is +9. If the spell is not on your class's spell list, you must make a DC 15 ability check using your spellcasting ability or the scroll is wasted.

title: Spell Scroll (6th Level)
description: A spell scroll bears the words of a single 6th-level spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can read the scroll and cast the spell at 6th level without providing any material components. The spell save DC is 17 and the attack bonus is +9. If the spell is not on your class's spell list, you must make a DC 16 ability check using your spellcasting ability or the scroll is wasted.

title: Spell Scroll (7th Level)
description: A spell scroll bears the words of a single 7th-level spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can read the scroll and cast the spell at 7th level without providing any material components. The spell save DC is 18 and the attack bonus is +10. If the spell is not on your class's spell list, you must make a DC 17 ability check using your spellcasting ability or the scroll is wasted.

title: Spell Scroll (8th Level)
description: A spell scroll bears the words of a single 8th-level spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can read the scroll and cast the spell at 8th level without providing any material components. The spell save DC is 18 and the attack bonus is +10. If the spell is not on your class's spell list, you must make a DC 18 ability check using your spellcasting ability or the scroll is wasted.

title: Spell Scroll (9th Level)
description: A spell scroll bears the words of a single 9th-level spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can read the scroll and cast the spell at 9th level without providing any material components. The spell save DC is 19 and the attack bonus is +11. If the spell is not on your class's spell list, you must make a DC 19 ability check using your spellcasting ability or the scroll is wasted.

title: Scroll of Protection
description: Using an action to read the scroll encloses you in an invisible barrier that extends from you to form a 5-foot-radius, 10-foot-high cylinder. For 5 minutes, creatures of a specified type (chosen when the scroll is created) cannot enter the cylinder or affect anything within it by any means. The barrier moves with you and the effect ends if you leave the barrier or use a melee attack through it.
